chunks.text: Elijah Polak is Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley's EECS department. His contact information includes office at 255M Cory Hall, phone 510-642-2644, and email polak@eecs.berkeley.edu. Office hours are by appointment only. He earned his B.S. from University of Melbourne in 1957, M.S. from UC Berkeley in 1959, and Ph.D. from UC Berkeley in 1961, all in Electrical Engineering. Since 1961, he has been on the faculty at UC Berkeley.
chunks.question: What is Elijah Polak's current position at UC Berkeley?

chunks.text: Elijah Polak has authored or co-authored over 200 papers and four books: 'Theory of Mathematical Programming and Optimal Control' (1970), 'Notes for a First Course on Linear Systems' (1970), 'Computational Methods in Optimization' (1971), and 'Optimization: Algorithms and Consistent Approximations' (1997). He also translated 'Absolute Stability of Regulator Systems' from Russian and co-edited 'System Theory' with L. A. Zadeh in 1969.
chunks.question: How many books has Elijah Polak authored or co-authored?

chunks.text: He was a Guggenheim Fellow in 1968-1969 at Institut Blaise Pascal in Paris and a UK Science Research Council Senior Postdoctoral Fellow at Imperial College, London multiple times between 1972 and 1990. He is a Life Fellow of IEEE, member of SIAM, and member of the Mathematical Programming Society. His research areas include Control, Intelligent Systems, Robotics (CIR) and numerical methods for engineering optimization.
chunks.question: Which fellowship did Elijah Polak receive in 1968-1969?

chunks.text: Selected publications include 'Optimization: Algorithms and Consistent Approximations' (1997), 'Computational Methods in Optimization: A Unified Approach' (1971), 'Note on the convergence of methods of conjugate directions' (1969), 'Constrained minimization under vector valued-criterial in finite dimensional spaces' (1967), and 'Constrained minimization problems in finite-dimensional spaces' (1966). Selected honors include IEEE Fellow (1977) and Guggenheim Fellow (1968).
chunks.question: When was Elijah Polak named an IEEE Fellow?
